replication_study.hypothesis: In the test phase of the bee categorization task, the mean 1D accuracy score will be higher in the contingent-information condition than in the full-information condition; equivalently, the difference in mean 1D scores (contingent minus full-information) will be greater than 0.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Replicating Rich & Gureckis (2018) – Prolific experiment dataset (analysis-data.csv), N≈474
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Experiment 1 analog; learning task with four binary features; analysis restricted to Test phase trials; conditions: contingent vs full-information
data_plan.sample_size: Approx. 474 participants (inferred from 15168 Test trials / 32 trials per participant in analysis-data.csv)
data_plan.access_details: Provided locally in /app/data/replication_data/analysis-data.csv; no access restrictions for replication execution.

qualification.explanation: This dataset is a new, larger online sample implementing the same experimental design and scoring (1D/2D strategy metrics) as the original Experiment 1. It provides an independent test of the focal claim under similar conditions, enabling a robust replication.
qualification.similarity_to_original: The dataset contains the same two feedback conditions (contingent vs full-information) and computes 1D and 2D-consistency measures used in the focal claim. Evidence: variables condition, correct_1d_a, correct_1d_b, and phase=='Test' are present in analysis-data.csv (replication_data/analysis-data.csv; columns listed via dataset info). The original claim mentions 1D scores and a credible interval for the difference (initial_details.txt). The paper summary confirms the same task structure, feedback regimes, and scoring (original_paper.pdf summary: bee task; test phase; 1D vs 2D scores).
qualification.deviation_from_original: Sample size and recruitment differ: the original Experiment 1 used N=101 participants (post_registration.json: sample_size), whereas the replication dataset targets ~474 Prolific participants (analysis.Rmd narrative and the row count in analysis-data.csv implies 15168 Test trials/32 = 474). The original paper reports Bayesian credible intervals from Stan-based models, whereas our replication script uses a frequentist independent-samples t-test with equal variances (to mirror analysis.Rmd) plus a bootstrap CI for the mean difference.

data_plan.notes: The dataset is trial-level with repeated measures per subject. The analysis aggregates to subject-level 1D scores in the Test phase (32 trials/subject). The columns correct_1d_a and correct_1d_b are boolean indicators of 1D rule consistency per trial. Phase is split into Learning and Test; we use only Test. Encoding is standard CSV; no unusual types detected. Condition labels are 'contingent' and 'full-information'.

steps: Compute subject-level 1D accuracy: for each subject and condition, acc_1d = max(mean(correct_1d_a), mean(correct_1d_b)) over 32 Test trials.
Summarize group means and SDs of acc_1d by condition.
Estimate the difference in means (contingent - full-information) and its 95% CI using equal-variance t distribution.
Run an independent-samples t-test with equal variances.
As a robustness check, compute a bootstrap 95% CI for the mean difference.
Save the subject-level summary and results to /app/data.
planned_method.models: Independent-samples t-test with equal variances; nonparametric bootstrap for CI robustness.
planned_method.outcome_variable: acc_1d (subject-level 1D accuracy at Test, computed as max of mean(correct_1d_a), mean(correct_1d_b))
planned_method.control_variables: None (random assignment; analysis at subject level)

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in means for acc_1d (contingent minus full-information), along with 95% CI
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Two-sample t-test with equal variances (scipy.stats.ttest_ind(equal_var=True)); bootstrap 95% CI for robustness

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion at the trial level if any 1D-correct flags are missing; compute subject-level scores only from valid Test trials. If a subject lacks any Test trials, exclude from analysis.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Primary criterion: 95% CI of mean difference excludes 0 and the direction is positive (contingent > full-information). Secondary: p < 0.05 in equal-variance t-test.

files.replication_data/analysis__py.py: Python analysis script. Reads /app/data/replication_data/analysis-data.csv, computes subject-level Test-phase 1D accuracy (acc_1d), summarizes by condition, performs an equal-variance t-test and computes 95% CIs (parametric and bootstrap). Outputs: /app/data/replication_test_summary.csv (subject-level scores) and /app/data/replication_results.json (test statistics and CIs). Set RNG via PY_SEED env var for bootstrap reproducibility.

codebase.notes: All file IO is constrained to /app/data. No external dependencies beyond pandas, numpy, and scipy. The analysis mirrors the Rmd logic for the focal test (equal variance t-test and 1D scoring) and adds a bootstrap CI to approximate the posterior CI directionality from the original paper.

instructions: Ensure the dataset is available at ./data/replication_data/analysis-data.csv (mounted to /app/data/replication_data/analysis-data.csv).
Run: python /app/data/original/24/input/replication_data/analysis__py.py if accessible, or copy the script into the container and run it.
Outputs will be saved to: /app/data/replication_test_summary.csv and /app/data/replication_results.json.
Assess whether the 95% CI for the mean difference (contingent - full-information) excludes 0 and is positive.
comparison_metrics: Mean 1D score in contingent vs full-information (target replication of pattern: contingent > full-information).
Difference in means (contingent - full-information) and its 95% CI; CI excluding 0 matches the original claim (initial_details.txt).
Effect size (Cohen’s d) as a standardized comparison.
Optional: bootstrap CI agreement with the parametric CI.
